sections.heading: 1 Introduction
sections.text:
Recurrent neural networks, long short-term memory
in particular, have been firmly established as state
transduction problems such as language modeling
efforts have since continued to push the boundaries
architectures [38, 24, 15].
Recurrent models typically factor computation along
sequences. Aligning the positions to steps in computation
states ht, as a function of the previous hidden state
sequential nature precludes parallelization within training
sequence lengths, as memory constraints limit batching
significant improvements in computational efficiency
computation [32], while also improving model performance
constraint of sequential computation, however, remains.
Attention mechanisms have become an integral part
tion models in various tasks, allowing modeling of
the input or output sequences [2, 19]. In all but a few
are used in conjunction with a recurrent network.
In this work we propose the Transformer, a model
relying entirely on an attention mechanism to draw
The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization

sections.heading: 5.1 Training Data and Batching
sections.text:
We trained on the standard WMT 2014 English-German
sentence pairs. Sentences were encoded using byte-pair
target vocabulary of about 37000 tokens. For English-French,
2014 English-French dataset consisting of 36M sentences

sections.heading: 7 Conclusion
sections.text:
In this work, we presented the Transformer, the first
attention, replacing the recurrent layers most commonly
multi-headed self-attention.
For translation tasks, the Transformer can be trained
on recurrent or convolutional layers. On both WMT
English-to-French translation tasks, we achieve a
model outperforms even all previously reported ensembles.
We are excited about the future of attention-based
plan to extend the Transformer to problems involving
to investigate local, restricted attention mechanisms
such as images, audio and video. Making generation
The code we used to train and evaluate our
tensorflow/tensor2tensor.
Acknowledgements We are grateful to Nal Kalchbrenner
comments, corrections and inspiration.
